content: Treatment based on severity, acuity, and volume status. Severe symptomatic (seizures, altered mental status): immediate 3% hypertonic saline 100-150mL IV bolus, repeat up to 3 times if needed; target increase 4-6 mEq/L in first few hours. Hypovolemic: isotonic saline to restore volume (suppresses ADH). Euvolemic (SIADH): fluid restriction (<800-1000mL/day), salt tablets, loop diuretics; vaptans (tolvaptan) for refractory cases; demeclocycline for chronic SIADH. Hypervolemic: fluid and sodium restriction, loop diuretics, treat underlying condition; vaptans in select cases. Correction rate critical: limit to 6-8 mEq/L in first 24 hours, <18 mEq/L in 48 hours to prevent osmotic demyelination syndrome (ODS). Higher risk of ODS if alcoholism, malnutrition, liver disease, hypokalemia. If overcorrection occurs, use DDAVP and D5W to re-lower sodium. Chronic asymptomatic: gradual correction with fluid restriction, address underlying cause.
